skill.name: difficult-workplace-conversations
skill.description: Helps users prepare, deliver, and follow up on difficult workplace conversations using structured communication frameworks.
skill.summary: Plan workplace conflict, feedback, performance, and sensitive conversations with practical scripts and follow-up steps.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, reconnaissance, and weak-crypto patterns, but the cited locations are markdown examples, conversation worksheets, and ordinary workplace guidance. No executable code, network access, environment access, prompt-injection attempt, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Difficult workplace conversations often fail because people enter them anxious, vague, or reactive. This skill turns sensitive discussions into prepared, respectful conversations with clear next steps.
actual_capabilities: Creates preparation plans that separate facts, interpretations, impact, and desired outcomes.
Drafts neutral opening lines for feedback, conflict, performance, compensation, and sensitive topics.
Suggests response phrases for defensiveness, denial, emotional reactions, and stalled conversations.
Builds follow-up plans with agreements, ownership, timelines, and check-in points.
Provides emotional regulation prompts for before, during, and after challenging discussions.
limitations: It does not replace legal, HR, medical, or mental health advice.
It cannot verify workplace facts or judge who is objectively correct.
It depends on the user providing accurate context and constraints.
High-risk situations may require HR, legal counsel, or formal escalation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Feedback for a Direct Report
use_cases.description: Plan a clear performance discussion with specific examples, a respectful opening, and measurable follow-up actions.

use_cases.title: Resolve Conflict With a Peer
use_cases.description: Turn a tense workplace incident into a structured conversation that separates facts, impact, and shared interests.

use_cases.title: Discuss Compensation or Promotion
use_cases.description: Prepare a sensitive upward conversation with evidence, goals, alternatives, and calm response options.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Conversation Prep
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: prepare a basic workplace discussion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me prepare for a difficult workplace conversation. The topic is [topic], the other person is [role], and my main concern is [concern]. Give me facts to clarify, a goal, and an opening line.

prompt_templates.title: Feedback Script Builder
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: turn feedback into a respectful script.
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to give feedback about [behavior] that affected [impact]. Help me write a neutral opening, an SBI-style message, listening questions, and a follow-up plan.

prompt_templates.title: Conflict Response Planner
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: handle defensive or emotional reactions.
prompt_templates.prompt: I expect this person may respond by [likely reaction]. Help me prepare phrases that acknowledge their view, return to facts, and keep the conversation productive.

prompt_templates.title: High-Stakes Conversation Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: plan a sensitive or repeated issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build a complete preparation, delivery, and follow-up strategy for [situation]. Include risks, escalation points, documentation needs, boundaries, and success measures.

output_examples.input: I need to talk to a teammate who missed three deadlines this month.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a preparation summary, a neutral opening, questions to understand root causes, and a follow-up agreement with dates.

output_examples.input: A colleague criticized my work in a meeting, and I am angry.
output_examples.output: The skill would help separate facts from interpretations, check emotional readiness, and draft a private conversation opener focused on impact.

output_examples.input: I want to ask my manager for a raise but feel nervous.
output_examples.output: The skill would organize accomplishments, market context, alternatives, likely objections, and a calm opening for the compensation discussion.

best_practices: Provide concrete facts, dates, behaviors, and impacts instead of broad judgments.
Use the preparation worksheet before asking for scripts or response phrases.
Ask for follow-up language that matches your role, authority, and workplace policies.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to avoid required HR, legal, safety, or compliance escalation.
Do not ask it to create manipulative scripts that pressure or shame another person.
Do not rely on generic advice when the situation involves harassment, threats, or discrimination.

faq.question: Can this skill write the exact words I should say?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft opening lines, feedback statements, response phrases, and follow-up messages based on your context.

faq.question: Does it support conversations with managers?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for upward feedback, compensation discussions, and requests for support or change.

faq.question: Can it help when emotions are high?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes preparation and pause techniques for anger, anxiety, defensiveness, and post-conversation processing.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for HR advice?
faq.answer: No. Use HR, legal, or safety channels when policy, law, harassment, threats, or formal discipline may be involved.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Share the situation, observed behavior, impact, relationship, desired outcome, risks, and any workplace constraints.

faq.question: Can it help after the conversation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create written summaries, check-in plans, next steps, and reflection prompts after the discussion.
